{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Rename sprint-related document types to week terminology
     2	-- Part of Sprint → Week rename refactor
     3	
     4	-- Rename document_type enum values.
     5	-- Some databases already include weekly_* labels via schema.sql, so handle mixed states.
     6	DO $$
     7	BEGIN
     8	  IF EXISTS (
     9	    SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    10	    WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'sprint_plan'
    11	  ) THEN
    12	    IF EXISTS (
    13	      SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    14	      WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'weekly_plan'
    15	    ) THEN
    16	      UPDATE documents
    17	      SET document_type = 'weekly_plan'::document_type
    18	      WHERE document_type = 'sprint_plan'::document_type;
    19	    ELSE
    20	      ALTER TYPE document_type RENAME VALUE 'sprint_plan' TO 'weekly_plan';
    21	    END IF;
    22	  END IF;
    23	
    24	  IF EXISTS (
    25	    SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    26	    WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'sprint_retro'
    27	  ) THEN
    28	    IF EXISTS (
    29	      SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    30	      WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'weekly_retro'
    31	    ) THEN
    32	      UPDATE documents
    33	      SET document_type = 'weekly_retro'::document_type
    34	      WHERE document_type = 'sprint_retro'::document_type;
    35	    ELSE
    36	      ALTER TYPE document_type RENAME VALUE 'sprint_retro' TO 'weekly_retro';
    37	    END IF;
    38	  END IF;
    39	
    40	  IF EXISTS (
    41	    SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    42	    WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'sprint_review'
    43	  ) THEN
    44	    IF EXISTS (
    45	      SELECT 1 FROM pg_enum
    46	      WHERE enumtypid = 'document_type'::regtype AND enumlabel = 'weekly_review'
    47	    ) THEN
    48	      UPDATE documents
    49	      SET document_type = 'weekly_review'::document_type
    50	      WHERE document_type = 'sprint_review'::document_type;
    51	    ELSE
    52	      ALTER TYPE document_type RENAME VALUE 'sprint_review' TO 'weekly_review';
    53	    END IF;
    54	  END IF;
    55	END
    56	$$;
    57	
    58	-- Note: We keep 'sprint' as a document_type because it represents the sprint document itself.
    59	-- The terminology change is "Sprint 3" → "Week of Jan 27" in UI, but the underlying
    60	-- document concept remains valid. The sprint document stores sprint_number and owner_id
    61	-- for derived 7-day windows.
    62	
    63	-- Update accountability_type values in issue properties
    64	-- Sprint-related accountability types become week-related
    65	UPDATE documents
    66	SET properties = jsonb_set(properties, '{accountability_type}', '"weekly_plan"')
    67	WHERE properties->>'accountability_type' = 'sprint_plan';
    68	
    69	UPDATE documents
    70	SET properties = jsonb_set(properties, '{accountability_type}', '"weekly_review"')
    71	WHERE properties->>'accountability_type' = 'sprint_review';
    72	
    73	UPDATE documents
    74	SET properties = jsonb_set(properties, '{accountability_type}', '"week_start"')
    75	WHERE properties->>'accountability_type' = 'sprint_start';
    76	
    77	UPDATE documents
    78	SET properties = jsonb_set(properties, '{accountability_type}', '"week_issues"')
    79	WHERE properties->>'accountability_type' = 'sprint_issues';
    80	","total_lines":80}

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Migration: Drop program_id column from documents table
     2	-- This completes the migration from direct column references to the document_associations
     3	-- junction table pattern. All program associations are now stored exclusively in
     4	-- document_associations with relationship_type = 'program'.
     5	--
     6	-- Prerequisites:
     7	-- - Migration 028 backfilled all program_id values to document_associations
     8	-- - All routes updated to read/write via document_associations (Story 2)
     9	-- - No code references d.program_id for reads
    10	--
    11	-- Post-migration:
    12	-- - program_id column will no longer exist
    13	-- - All queries use document_associations JOIN for program lookup
    14	-- - Pattern is now consistent with project_id and sprint_id (dropped in 027)
    15	--
    16	-- This migration is idempotent - it checks for column existence before operating.
    17	-- This is necessary because schema.sql may not include the legacy program_id column.
    18	
    19	DO $$
    20	DECLARE
    21	  orphan_count INTEGER;
    22	  backfilled_count INTEGER;
    23	  column_exists BOOLEAN;
    24	BEGIN
    25	  -- Check if program_id column exists
    26	  SELECT EXISTS (
    27	    SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
    28	    WHERE table_name = 'documents' AND column_name = 'program_id'
    29	  ) INTO column_exists;
    30	
    31	  IF column_exists THEN
    32	    -- Self-healing backfill: Fix any orphaned program_id values before drop
    33	    -- This handles cases where documents were created between migration 028 and 029
    34	    EXECUTE '
    35	      INSERT INTO document_associations (document_id, related_id, relationship_type, metadata)
    36	      SELECT
    37	        d.id AS document_id,
    38	        d.program_id AS related_id,
    39	        ''program''::relationship_type AS relationship_type,
    40	        jsonb_build_object(
    41	          ''backfilled_from'', ''program_id_column'',
    42	          ''backfilled_at'', NOW(),
    43	          ''migration'', ''029_drop_program_id_column_self_heal''
    44	        )
    45	      FROM documents d
    46	      WHERE d.program_id IS NOT NULL
    47	        AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
    48	        AND NOT EXISTS (
    49	          SELECT 1 FROM document_associations da
    50	          WHERE da.document_id = d.id
    51	            AND da.relationship_type = ''program''
    52	        )
    53	      ON CONFLICT (document_id, related_id, relationship_type) DO NOTHING
    54	    ';
    55	
    56	    -- Count what we just backfilled
    57	    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO backfilled_count
    58	    FROM document_associations
    59	    WHERE metadata->>'migration' = '029_drop_program_id_column_self_heal';
    60	
    61	    IF backfilled_count > 0 THEN
    62	      RAISE NOTICE 'Self-healed % orphaned program associations', backfilled_count;
    63	    END IF;
    64	
    65	    -- Final check - should be 0 now
    66	    EXECUTE '
    67	      SELECT COUNT(*) FROM documents d
    68	      WHERE d.program_id IS NOT NULL
    69	        AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
    70	        AND NOT EXISTS (
    71	          SELECT 1 FROM document_associations da
    72	          WHERE da.document_id = d.id
    73	            AND da.relationship_type = ''program''
    74	        )
    75	    ' INTO orphan_count;
    76	
    77	    IF orphan_count > 0 THEN
    78	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Self-heal failed: % documents still have orphaned program_id', orphan_count;
    79	    END IF;
    80	
    81	    RAISE NOTICE 'Pre-flight check passed: All program associations verified';
    82	  ELSE
    83	    RAISE NOTICE 'program_id column does not exist - skipping self-heal backfill';
    84	  END IF;
    85	END
    86	$$;
    87	
    88	-- Drop index first (if exists)
    89	DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_documents_program_id;
    90	
    91	-- Drop the program_id column (IF EXISTS makes this idempotent)
    92	ALTER TABLE documents DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS program_id;
    93	
    94	-- Log completion
    95	DO $$
    96	BEGIN
    97	  RAISE NOTICE 'Dropped program_id column from documents table (if it existed)';
    98	  RAISE NOTICE 'Program associations now managed exclusively via document_associations table';
    99	  RAISE NOTICE 'Pattern now consistent with project_id and sprint_id (dropped in migration 027)';
   100	END
   101	$$;
   102	","total_lines":102}

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Base database schema for Ship's document model
     2	
     3	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
     4	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     5	
     6	DO $$
     7	BEGIN
     8	  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_type WHERE typname = 'document_type') THEN
     9	    CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM (
    10	      'wiki',
    11	      'issue',
    12	      'program',
    13	      'project',
    14	      'sprint',
    15	      'person',
    16	      'weekly_plan',
    17	      'weekly_retro',
    18	      'standup',
    19	      'weekly_review'
    20	    );
    21	  END IF;
    22	END
    23	$$;
    24	
    25	DO $$
    26	BEGIN
    27	  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_type WHERE typname = 'relationship_type') THEN
    28	    CREATE TYPE relationship_type AS ENUM ('parent', 'project', 'sprint', 'program');
    29	  END IF;
    30	END
    31	$$;
    32	
    33	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    34	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    35	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    36	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    37	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    38	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    39	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    40	);
    41	
    42	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    43	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    44	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    45	  password_hash TEXT,
    46	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    47	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    48	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    49	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,
    50	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    51	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50),
    52	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    53	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    54	);
    55	
    56	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    57	
    58	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    59	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    60	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    61	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    62	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    63	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    64	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    65	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    66	);
    67	
    68	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    69	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    70	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    71	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    72	  token TEXT UNIQUE,
    73	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    74	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    75	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,
    76	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    77	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    78	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    79	);
    80	
    81	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    82	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    83	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    84	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    85	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    86	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    87	  resource_type TEXT,
    88	  resource_id UUID,
    89	  details JSONB,
    90	  ip_address INET,
    91	  user_agent TEXT,
    92	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    93	);
    94	
    95	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    96	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    97	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
   100	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   101	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   102	  user_agent TEXT,
   103	  ip_address TEXT
   104	);
   105	
   106	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
   107	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   108	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
   109	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
   110	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   111	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
   112	);
   113	
   114	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   115	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   116	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   117	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   118	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   119	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}'::jsonb,
   120	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
   124	  ticket_number INTEGER,
   125	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   126	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   127	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   128	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   129	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   130	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   131	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   132	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   133	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   134	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   135	  original_type VARCHAR(50),
   136	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   137	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   138	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   139	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   140	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   141	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id),
   142	  CONSTRAINT documents_ticket_number_positive CHECK (ticket_number IS NULL OR ticket_number > 0)
   143	);
   144	
   145	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_associations (
   146	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   147	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   148	  related_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   149	  relationship_type relationship_type NOT NULL,
   150	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   151	  metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
   152	  CONSTRAINT unique_association UNIQUE (document_id, related_id, relationship_type),
   153	  CONSTRAINT no_self_reference CHECK (document_id != related_id)
   154	);
   155	
   156	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_history (
   157	  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   158	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   159	  field TEXT NOT NULL,
   160	  old_value TEXT,
   161	  new_value TEXT,
   162	  changed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   163	  automated_by TEXT,
   164	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   165	);
   166	
   167	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_snapshots (
   168	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   169	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170	  document_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   171	  title TEXT NOT NULL,
   172	  properties JSONB,
   173	  ticket_number INTEGER,
   174	  snapshot_reason VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'conversion',
   175	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   176	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
   177	);
   178	
   179	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_tokens (
   180	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   181	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   182	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   183	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   184	  token_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
   185	  token_prefix TEXT NOT NULL,
   186	  last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   187	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   188	  revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   189	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   190	  UNIQUE(user_id, workspace_id, name)
   191	);
   192	
   193	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sprint_iterations (
   194	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   195	  sprint_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   196	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   197	  story_id TEXT,
   198	  story_title TEXT NOT NULL,
   199	  status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pass', 'fail', 'in_progress')),
   200	  what_attempted TEXT,
   201	  blockers_encountered TEXT,
   202	  author_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   203	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   204	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
   205	);
   206	
   207	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issue_iterations (
   208	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
     6	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     7	
     8	-- Workspaces
     9	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    10	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    11	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    12	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    13	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    14	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    15	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    16	);
    17	
    18	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    19	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    20	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    21	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    22	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    23	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    24	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    25	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    26	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    27	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    28	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    29	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    30	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    31	);
    32	
    33	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    34	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    35	
    36	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    37	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    38	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    39	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    40	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    41	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    43	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    44	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    46	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    47	);
    48	
    49	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    50	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    51	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    52	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    53	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    54	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    55	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    56	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    57	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    58	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    59	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    60	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    61	);
    62	
    63	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    64	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    65	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    66	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    67	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    68	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    69	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    70	  resource_type TEXT,
    71	  resource_id UUID,
    72	  details JSONB,
    73	  ip_address INET,
    74	  user_agent TEXT,
    75	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    76	);
    77	
    78	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    79	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    80	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    81	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    82	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    84	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    85	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    87	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    88	  user_agent TEXT,
    89	  ip_address TEXT
    90	);
    91	
    92	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    93	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    94	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    95	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    97	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    98	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    99	);
   100	
   101	-- Document types enum
   102	DO $$ BEGIN
   103	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   104	EXCEPTION
   105	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   106	END $$;
   107	
   108	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   109	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   110	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   111	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   112	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   113	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   114	
   115	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   116	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}'::jsonb,
   117	
   118	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   119	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   120	
   121	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   122	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   123	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   124	
   125	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   126	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   127	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   128	
   129	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   130	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   131	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   132	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   133	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   134	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
   135	
   136	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   137	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   138	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   139	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   140	
   141	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   142	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   143	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   144	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   145	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   146	
   147	  -- Document conversion tracking
   148	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   149	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   150	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   151	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   152	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   153	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   154	
   155	  -- Timestamps
   156	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   158	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   159	
   160	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   161	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   162	
   163	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   164	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   165	);
   166	
   167	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   168	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   169	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   170	DECLARE
   171	  current_parent UUID;
   172	  depth INT := 0;
   173	  max_depth INT := 100;
   174	BEGIN
   175	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   176	    RETURN NEW;
   177	  END IF;
   178	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   179	    RETURN NEW;
   180	  END IF;
   181	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   182	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   183	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   184	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   185	    END IF;
   186	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   187	    depth := depth + 1;
   188	  END LOOP;
   189	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   190	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   191	  END IF;
   192	  RETURN NEW;
   193	END;
   194	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   195	
   196	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON

     2	 * Welcome to Ship - Tutorial Document
     3	 *
     4	 * This is the comprehensive onboarding document shown to new users.
     5	 * Used by both seed.ts (dev data) and setup.ts (production first-run).
     6	 */
     7	
     8	export const WELCOME_DOCUMENT_TITLE = 'Welcome to Ship';
     9	
    10	export const WELCOME_DOCUMENT_CONTENT = {
    11	  type: 'doc',
    12	  content: [
    13	    // Introduction
    14	    {
    15	      type: 'paragraph',
    16	      content: [
    17	        { type: 'text', text: 'Ship helps your team track work, plan sprints, and write documentation—all in one place. Jump to the section that matches your role:' },
    18	      ],
    19	    },
    20	    {
    21	      type: 'bulletList',
    22	      content: [
    23	        {
    24	          type: 'listItem',
    25	          content: [{

    27	            content: [

    29	              { type: 'text', text: ' — Track issues, manage sprints, update status' },
    30	            ],
    31	          }],
    32	        },
    33	        {
    34	          type: 'listItem',
    35	          content: [{
    36	            type: 'paragraph',
    37	            content: [
    38	              { type: 'text', marks: [{ type: 'bold' }], text: 'For Program Managers' },
    39	              { type: 'text', text: ' — Write specs, organize programs, plan sprints' },
    40	            ],
    41	          }],
    42	        },
    43	        {
    44	          type: 'listItem',
    45	          content: [{
    46	            type: 'paragraph',
    47	            content: [
    48	              { type: 'text', marks: [{ type: 'bold' }], text: 'For Executives' },
    49	              { type: 'text', text: ' — See delivery progress, team workload, and accountability' },
    50	            ],
    51	          }],
    52	        },
    53	      ],
    54	    },
    55	
    56	    // ============ FOR DEVELOPERS ============

{"success":true,"content":"     1	# Database Verification Checklist
     2	
     3	This artifact records the database setup verification expected for a local PostgreSQL run.
     4	
     5	## Intended validation steps
     6	
     7	1. Start a local PostgreSQL instance.
     8	2. Run migrations:
     9	   - `pnpm db:migrate`
    10	3. Run seeds:
    11	   - `pnpm db:seed`
    12	4. Inspect the created schema with `\dt`, `\d <table>`, or equivalent SQL metadata queries.
    13	5. Confirm the schema tracking table and seeded content are present.
    14	
    15	## Schema entry points
    16	
    17	- Migration runner: `api/src/db/migrate.ts`
    18	- Schema bootstrap: `api/src/db/bootstrap.ts`
    19	- Seed script: `api/src/db/seed.ts`
    20	- Schema definition: `api/src/db/schema.sql`
    21	
    22	## Tables observed in schema.sql
    23	
    24	- `schema_migrations`
    25	- `workspaces`
    26	- `users`
    27	- `workspace_memberships`
    28	- `workspace_invites`
    29	- `audit_logs`
    30	- `sessions`
    31	- `oauth_state`
    32	- `documents`
    33	- `document_associations`
    34	- `document_history`
    35	- `document_snapshots`
    36	- `api_tokens`
    37	- `feedback`
    38	- `sprints`
    39	- `weekly_templates`
    40	- `weekly_plans`
    41	- `weekly_retros`
    42	- `standups`
    43	- `weekly_reviews`
    44	- `comments`
    45	- `shipyard_runs`
    46	- `shipyard_messages`
    47	- `shipyard_contexts`
    48	- FleetGraph tables introduced by later migrations
    49	
    50	## Verification status in this run
    51	
    52	- Local PostgreSQL Docker container was **not available** in the execution environment, so live migration/seed execution could not be completed here.
    53	- The repo does include a migration runner that applies `schema.sql` plus numbered migrations and records them in `schema_migrations`.
    54	- The seed script reuses the same bootstrap path, so a successful seed run implies schema readiness plus seeded demo rows.
    55	
    56	## Recommended manual confirmation output
    57	
    58	After starting PostgreSQL locally, capture:
    59	
    60	- migration output from `pnpm db:migrate`
    61	- seed output from `pnpm db:seed`
    62	- table list from `psql -d <db> -c '\dt'`
    63	- selected column descriptions from `psql -d <db> -c '\d workspaces'` and similar
    64	
    65	## Quick SQL checks
    66	
    67	```sql
    68	SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = 'public' ORDER BY tablename;
    69	SELECT * FROM schema_migrations ORDER BY version;
    70	SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;
    71	SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces;
    72	SELECT COUNT(*) FROM documents;
    73	```
    74	","total_lines":74}

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
     6	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     7	
     8	-- Workspaces
     9	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    10	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    11	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    12	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    13	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    14	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    15	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    16	);
    17	
    18	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    19	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    20	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    21	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    22	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    23	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    24	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    25	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    26	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    27	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    28	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    29	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    30	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    31	);
    32	
    33	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    34	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    35	
    36	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    37	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    38	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    39	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    40	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    41	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    43	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    44	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    46	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    47	);
    48	
    49	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    50	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    51	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    52	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    53	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    54	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    55	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    56	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    57	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    58	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    59	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    60	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    61	);
    62	
    63	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    64	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    65	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    66	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    67	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    68	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    69	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    70	  resource_type TEXT,
    71	  resource_id UUID,
    72	  details JSONB,
    73	  ip_address INET,
    74	  user_agent TEXT,
    75	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    76	);
    77	
    78	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    79	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    80	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    81	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    82	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    84	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    85	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    87	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    88	  user_agent TEXT,
    89	  ip_address TEXT
    90	);
    91	
    92	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    93	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    94	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    95	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    97	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    98	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    99	);
   100	
   101	-- Document types enum
   102	DO $$ BEGIN
   103	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   104	EXCEPTION
   105	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   106	END $$;
   107	
   108	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   109	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   110	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   111	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   112	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   113	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   114	
   115	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   116	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}'::jsonb,
   117	
   118	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   119	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   120	
   121	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   122	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   123	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   124	
   125	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   126	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   127	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   128	
   129	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   130	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   131	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   132	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   133	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   134	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
   135	
   136	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   137	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   138	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   139	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   140	
   141	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   142	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   143	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   144	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   145	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   146	
   147	  -- Document conversion tracking
   148	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   149	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   150	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   151	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   152	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   153	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   154	
   155	  -- Timestamps
   156	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   158	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   159	
   160	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   161	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   162	
   163	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   164	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   165	);
   166	
   167	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   168	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   169	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   170	DECLARE
   171	  current_parent UUID;
   172	  depth INT := 0;
   173	  max_depth INT := 100;
   174	BEGIN
   175	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   176	    RETURN NEW;
   177	  END IF;
   178	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   179	    RETURN NEW;
   180	  END IF;
   181	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   182	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   183	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   184	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   185	    END IF;
   186	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   187	    depth := depth + 1;
   188	  END LOOP;
   189	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   190	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   191	  END IF;
   192	  RETURN NEW;
   193	END;
   194	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   195	
   196	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON

messages.content:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns.
